name: Devesa da Rogueira Trail
name_original: Pico Formigueiros y Devesa da Rogueira – circular desde Moreda – Alto de Couto
description: Hike the Devesa da Rogueira Trail, a moderate 7.6-mile route in the Courel Mountains UNESCO Global Geopark, known for its diverse forests an
region_intro: Hiking around Folgoso Do Courel offers access to mountainous terrain, diverse forests, and significant geological features within the Courel Mountains UNESCO Global Geopark. The region is characterized by rounded, steep-sided mountains composed of slate, schist, and sandstone, with its highest point at Pico Formigueiros. Numerous rivers, cascades, and waterfalls sculpt the landscape, alongside ancient chestnut groves and native forests like Devesa da Rogueira. Folgoso Do Courel hiking terrain includes varied elevations and natural water features. Best hiking trails in Folgoso Do Courel The most popular hiking route is Devesa da Rogueira Trail, a 7.6 miles (12.2 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses a biodiverse native forest known for its varied ecosystems. Another top favourite among local hikers is Lagoa da Lucenza Route, a difficult 10.7 miles (17.2 km) path. This route features a glacial lake and impressive waterfalls, with significant elevation gain. Local hikers also love the Devesa da Rogueira – Deer Spring Waterfall loop from Centro de Interpretación - Aula da Natureza, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail leading through the Devesa da Rogueira forest, often completed in about 4 hours. Hiking around Folgoso Do Courel is defined by mountainous terrain, diverse native forests, and numerous water features. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate forest loops to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Folgoso Do Courel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Folgoso Do Courel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Folgoso Do Courel?
A: Folgoso Do Courel offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 10 easy, 14 moderate, and 6 difficult options, allowing everyone to explore the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Folgoso Do Courel?
A: The terrain in Folgoso Do Courel is predominantly mountainous, characterized by rounded, steep-sided formations composed of slate, schist, and sandstone. You'll encounter diverse native forests, ancient chestnut groves, and numerous water features like rivers, cascades, and waterfalls, all within the Courel Mountains UNESCO Global Geopark.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls in Folgoso Do Courel?
A: Yes, the region is known for its beautiful water features. You can find waterfalls like the Fervenza da Albardaira and Fervenza do Rexío mentioned in the region research. For a specific route, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38156424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devesa da Rogueira – Deer Spring Waterfall loop from Centro de Interpretación - Aula da Natureza&lt;/a&gt;, which includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5304025" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deer Spring Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Folgoso Do Courel?
A: Yes, many routes in Folgoso Do Courel are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38156424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devesa da Rogueira – Deer Spring Waterfall loop from Centro de Interpretación - Aula da Natureza&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 10.5 km loop through the biodiverse Devesa da Rogueira forest.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Folgoso Do Courel is rich in natural and historical landmarks. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5304025" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deer Spring Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6560211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lor River&lt;/a&gt;, and ancient chestnut groves. The region is also part of a UNESCO Global Geopark, featuring unique geological formations and viewpoints like Mirador de Pico Polín and Mirador de Pena do Escribano.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Folgoso Do Courel?
A: The hiking trails in Folgoso Do Courel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning mountain landscapes, the rich biodiversity of forests like Devesa da Rogueira, and the impressive water features, including glacial lakes and waterfalls.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Folgoso Do Courel offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979709118/lagoa-da-lucenza-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagoa da Lucenza Route&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 17.2 km path featuring a glacial lake, Pico Formigueiros (the highest point at 1654 meters), and impressive waterfalls, with significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find trails that explore the region's historical or cultural heritage?
A: Absolutely. Folgoso Do Courel has a rich cultural heritage. Trails like the Ruta PR-G 220 (Baldomir to Castro da Torre) combine nature with historical sites, passing through traditional villages and ancient fortified settlements known as 'castros.' The Ruta do Río Pequeño (PR-G 219) explores ancient ironworks and rural villages, offering insights into local culture.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate routes suitable for a more relaxed walk?
A: Yes, Folgoso Do Courel provides a good selection of easier routes. For a moderate option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979717164/devesa-da-rogueira-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devesa da Rogueira Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 12.2 km path through a biodiverse native forest. Another moderate choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008183" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panorama – Church of San Xoán de Hospital loop from Padornelo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 16.75 km long.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Folgoso Do Courel?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the diverse forests and water features suggest that spring and autumn would be ideal for hiking in Folgoso Do Courel. Spring offers lush greenery and flowing waterfalls, while autumn brings vibrant foliage to the chestnut groves and native woodlands. Summers can be warm, and winters may present challenges with higher elevations and potential snow.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain of Folgoso Do Courel provides numerous viewpoints. The region research mentions Mirador de Pico Polín, which offers panoramic views of the Lor valley, and Mirador de Pena do Escribano, known for its dramatic vertical drop. Many trails will lead to elevated points with expansive vistas of the Courel Mountains.
tips: Waterfalls located at an altitude of 1264 meters in Folgoso do Courel that fall into Rego da Rogueira. The waterfalls present a curious bicolor appearance due to the wear of the wall from which they descend in parallel with different types of water: crystalline waters and ferruginous waters.

At the highest point of the Courel mountain range is the Formigueiros peak, at an altitude of 1,639 meters. The ascent through low difficulty trails leads to the top where you can see a beautiful panoramic view of Teso das Papoulas, Pico Polín and Devesa da Rogueira.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Devesa da Rogueira Trail, and is it suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: The Devesa da Rogueira Trail is generally considered to have a medium to high difficulty, making it more suitable for experienced hikers. The main route involves a significant and often intense ascent, with steep gradients and sections on slate slabs, especially challenging when wet. It is not typically recommended for beginners or young families due to its demanding nature and elevation gain of nearly 900 meters.

faqs.question: Is there a recommended starting point or alternative for a less strenuous hike?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the main route often starts with a challenging ascent from the Nature Classroom in Moreda, a shorter and less strenuous path is available from Alto do Couto. This alternative bypasses the hardest initial section, offering a more accessible entry to the trail's beautiful scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park when visiting the Devesa da Rogueira Trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is typically available near the Nature Classroom in Moreda, which serves as a common starting point for the trail. If opting for the less strenuous start, look for parking options around Alto do Couto.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Devesa da Rogueira Trail or the Courel Mountains UNESCO Global Geopark?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Devesa da Rogueira Trail or to access the Courel Mountains UNESCO Global Geopark. The area is open to the public for exploration.

faqs.question: What are the regulations for hiking within the Courel Mountains UNESCO Global Geopark?
faqs.answer: As part of a UNESCO Global Geopark, visitors are encouraged to practice responsible tourism. This includes staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife or plants, taking all litter with you, and respecting local communities and their traditions. Camping is generally restricted to designated areas, and open fires are prohibited.

faqs.question: What are the main natural highlights or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in natural beauty. Key highlights include the unique Deer Spring Waterfall (Fontes do Cervo), where two distinct springs emerge. You'll also pass through one of Spain's most botanically diverse forests, with ancient chestnuts, oaks, and beeches. For panoramic views, the trail offers vistas from stony ledges under Monte do Cabeza do Couto and expansive views from viewpoints like Pico Polín. The route can also be extended to reach Pico Formigueiros, the highest peak in Serra do Courel, for breathtaking sweeping views.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Devesa da Rogueira Trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding. You can expect strong, zigzagging inclines, especially at the start, with some sections on slate slabs. The path winds through dense native forests, pastoral landscapes, and can be steep and uncomfortable on descents. Sturdy hiking boots are essential due to the uneven and sometimes slippery surfaces.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike the Devesa da Rogueira Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is beautiful year-round, but autumn is particularly stunning when the beech forests display vibrant colors. Spring offers lush greenery and blooming flora. Summer provides warmer, drier conditions, though the ascents can be strenuous. Winter can be challenging with snow and wet conditions, requiring appropriate gear and caution.

faqs.question: Is the Devesa da Rogueira Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs are not explicitly stated, it is generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect the diverse wildlife and flora of this ecological jewel. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a challenging hike and bring plenty of water for them.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Due to the demanding terrain and steep gradients, it is highly recommended to wear sturdy hiking boots. You should also bring sufficient provisions, plenty of water, and consider using hiking poles for stability, especially on ascents and descents. Layered clothing is advisable, as weather conditions can change.

faqs.question: Does the Devesa da Rogueira Trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Devesa da Rogueira Trail (which is largely covered by the PR-G 222) intersects with other paths. The route also passes through sections of the Ruta da Devesa da Rogueira, and smaller portions of the Ruta da Lagoa da Lucenza and the Ruta do Monte Cido.
